# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class MarshalCookieSessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore
  COOKIE_NAME = :session

  def initialize(app, options = {})
    super
    @cookie_options = options.slice(:domain, :path, :secure, :httponly, :same_site, :expires)
  end

  private

  def get_session(env, sid)
    req = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    raw = req.cookie_jar[COOKIE_NAME]
    data = decode_session(raw)

    session = data.is_a?(Hash) ? data : {}
    session = session.dup
    session["session_id"] ||= sid&.public_id || generate_sid.to_s

    [session["session_id"], session]
  rescue => _e
    sid = generate_sid
    [sid.public_id, { "session_id" => sid.public_id }]
  end

  def set_session(env, sid, session_data, options)
    req = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    raw = encode_session(session_data)

    cookie_options = @cookie_options.merge(options)
    req.cookie_jar[COOKIE_NAME] = cookie_options.merge(value: raw)

    sid
  rescue => _e
    false
  end

  def destroy_session(env, sid, options)
    req = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    req.cookie_jar.delete(COOKIE_NAME, options)
    generate_sid
  end

  def encode_session(data)
    Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(data))
  end

  def decode_session(raw)
    return {} if raw.nil? || raw.empty?
    Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(raw))
  rescue => _e
    {}
  end
end
